paper.title: Hypervelocity Stars in the Gaia era. Revisiting the most extreme stars from the MMT survey

extraction.summary: Identified 22 stars that the paper treats as possibly unbound from the Milky Way: 19 HVS stars (HVS1,4,5,6,7,8,9,10,12,13,15,16,17,18,19,20,21,22,24) and 3 originally bound stars (B485, B1085, B576) that the paper indicates may be unbound, based on low bound probabilities in at least one Galactic potential model.

method_chain.summary: Input data: MMT HVS survey spectra (provided by Brown), Gaia DR2 astrometry (proper motions, positions), HST proper motions for subsample (HVS1,10,12,13).

source_refs.context: Gaia DR2 proper motions and HST proper motions

method_chain.summary: Sample selection: 40 stars with available MMT spectra out of 42 high-velocity outliers; 37 with complete astrometry for kinematic analysis.

source_refs.context: extend analysis to 40 out of 42; complete astrometric data for 37

method_chain.summary: Astrometric quality: RUWE < 1.4 from ARI Gaia Data Service.
Spectral analysis: fit flux-calibrated MMT spectra with NLTE model atmospheres (ADS grid) to derive Teff, log g, v_sini, and stellar mass, age, radius from evolutionary tracks.
SED fitting: construct SEDs from photometry, fit angular diameter and E(B-V) using synthetic photometry with fixed spectroscopic parameters.
Spectrophotometric distance: d = 2R / theta, where radius R from mass and log g, angular diameter theta from SED fit.
Solar position: Galactocentric distance R0 = 8.3 kpc (as shown in figures). Solar peculiar motion not explicitly reported.

source_refs.context: Three black circles ... solar radius (dashed; 8.3 kpc)

method_chain.summary: Compute Galactocentric Cartesian positions and velocities (v_x, v_y, v_z) and Galactic rest-frame velocity v_Grf.
Two Milky Way mass models: Model I (revised Allen & Santillan 1991) and Model II (Wilkinson & Evans 1999).
Calculate local Galactic escape velocity and bound probability P_b.
Backward orbit integration (up to 15 Gyr) with Monte Carlo to derive plane-crossing positions, pericenter, apocenter, and other orbital parameters.
Categorize stars into origin categories (unconstrained, outer rim, disk) based on plane-crossing contours.
Radial velocity derived from the same spectral fitting process as stellar parameters (simultaneous fit of flux-calibrated MMT spectra).

source_refs.context: four fitting parameters: Teff, log g, v_sini, and radial velocity

method_chain.summary: Extract origin metrics (ejection velocity, flight time) from the results of orbit integration, to be used in origin classification.

source_refs.context: ejection velocity v_ej,p ... the Galactic rest-frame velocity at plane intersection corrected for Galactic rotation

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS1 is one of the 19 HVS stars from the MMT sample reanalyzed. Its bound probability is 0% in both Galactic potential models, indicating it is unbound.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: The paper explicitly includes HVS1 in the kinematic sample, provides detailed bound assessment, and identifies it as likely unbound.

source_refs.context: Kinematic quantities for the program stars based on Model I.
the boundness probability of all stars except B1080 ... is lower than 50%
the origin of 19 stars in the sample is not really constrained

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS21 is originally classified as hypervelocity star. Reanalysis gives P_b = 0% in both Galactic mass models, strongly indicating unbound status. Included as a HVS candidate.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Bound probability 0% from paper tables unequivocally indicates unbound.

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS22 is the most extreme star with v_Grf = 1530 km/s, P_b = 0% in both models.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Extreme velocity and P_b=0% indicate unbound.

source_refs.context: With a current Galactic rest-frame velocity of 1530^{+690}_{-560} km/s, HVS22 is the star with the most outstanding kinematic properties
place of origin is completely unconstrained

inclusion_assessment.summary: HVS24 is originally classified as hypervelocity star. Reanalysis gives P_b = 33% (Model I) / 8% (Model II), possibly bound but still considered a HVS candidate by the paper.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Paper includes it among HVS stars re-evaluated; bound probability not conclusive.

source_refs.context: It is worthwhile to also reconsider the stars originally considered unbound.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B485 is a program star that may be unbound in the lighter mass model (Model II) with P_b = 2%. Included as possibly unbound, consistent with paper discussion.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Bound probability is low (2%) only in one model; included as possibly unbound per paper discussion.

source_refs.context: With P_b = 2%, B485 and B1085 would likely be unbound in the lighter mass model II.

inclusion_assessment.summary: B1085 is a program star that may be unbound in Model II with P_b = 2%.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Low bound probability (2%) only in Model II; included as possibly unbound per paper discussion.

source_refs.context: With P_b = 2%, B485 and B1085 would likely be unbound in the lighter mass model II.
stars either stem from the outer rim of the Galactic disk or not from the disk at all

inclusion_assessment.summary: B576 is originally bound, but if MS it would be unbound with P_b=0%. Paper discusses it as likely BHB star, but candidate included because of explicit unbound statement assuming MS nature.
inclusion_assessment.confidence_reason: Unbound only under MS assumption, but paper explicitly states unbound for that case.

source_refs.context: B576 would be clearly unbound (P_b=0%) in both Galactic potentials if it were a MS star.
if MS ... extragalactic; if BHB ... GC origin
if MS ... extragalactic; if BHB ... GC origin

candidate_groups_considered.description: Stars originally classified as bound (B designation) in the MMT HVS survey (Brown et al. 2014). The paper provides kinematic reanalysis and bound probabilities.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: Only B485, B1085, and B576 are included as possible unbound candidates because they have low bound probability in at least one model (P_b < 5\% for B485 and B1085 in Model II, or P_b=0\% for B576 if MS). Other B stars (e.g., B095, B129, B143, etc.) have high bound probability (mostly >5\% in both models) and the paper considers them bound.

source_refs.context: Except for three stars (B485, B576, B1085), all stars originally considered bound likely remain bound ...

candidate_groups_considered.description: Stars originally classified as unbound (HVS designation). All 19 HVS stars with full kinematics are included as possibly unbound.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper re-evaluates bound probabilities and finds some have non-zero bound probability but none are conclusively reclassified as bound; they remain possible HVS candidates.

source_refs.context: It is worthwhile to also reconsider the stars originally considered unbound ...
